2026-04-23T03:50:58.1403122Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1403774Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1404511Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1404915Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1405272Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1405530Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1405704Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1405900Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1406081Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1406333Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1406548Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1406855Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T03:50:58.1407132Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

provisioning libraries don't require an `AzureCredential` in the same way but should note the `azd` / `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` usage pattern\n- **Empty \"Next steps\" section** — No links to related samples, Azure documentation for Application Gateway for Containers, or the `Azure.Provisioning` README\n- **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource but the README Examples section has no example or mention of it; a snippet showing how to create and attach a security policy to a traffic controller is missing\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` (edit)\n\n1. **Fill in \"Authenticate the client\" section** — Replace the empty body under `### Authenticate the Client` with content similar to the pattern used in other provisioning packages (e.g., `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`):\n\n```markdown\n### Authenticate the Client\n\nThis library provisions Azure infrastructure using [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview). Authentication is handled automatically by `azd` during deployment. For local development or CI/CD, you can use `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` or any other credential from the [`Azure.Identity`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Identity) package.\n```\n\n2. **Fill in \"Next steps\" section** — Replace the empty body under `## Next steps` with useful links:\n\n```markdown\n## Next steps\n\n- [Azure Application Gateway for Containers documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/application-gateway/for-containers/overview)\n- [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\n- [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview)\n```\n\n3. **Add an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` example** — After the existing `### Create a Traffic Controller` example, add:\n\n```markdown\n### Create a Security Policy\n\nThis example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and associate it with a Traffic Controller.\n\n```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n**File to create:** TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\n                {\n                    Tags = { [\"environment\"] = \"test\" },\n                };\n            infra.Add(controller);\n\n CreateSecurityPolicy()\n{\n    await using Trycep test = CreateSecurityPolicyTest();\n    // Expected Bicep output verified by test runner\n    test.Compare(\n        \"\"\"\n        @description('The location for the resource(s) to be deployed.')\n        param location string = resourceGroup().location\n\n        resource controller 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers@2025-01-01' = {\n          name: take('controller-${uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24)\n          location: location\n        }\n\n        resource policy
